summary:
Proton beam therapy is indeed an established form of radiation treatment for craniopharyngioma, as confirmed by recent literature citing it alongside conventional therapies. Its primary advantage lies in reduced dose to surrounding sensitive brain tissues such as the optic chiasm and hypothalamus, which minimizes long-term complications especially important given the tumor’s location near these areas. Based solely on provided content, no further analysis or external knowledge was required beyond confirming explicit mentions within medical guidelines.

The summary above contains more than two sentences. Please provide a revised version strictly adhering to exactly two sentences:

Proton beam therapy is recognized as a valid option for treating craniopharyngioma, commonly included alongside standard radiotherapy approaches. It offers superior sparing of adjacent critical neural structures compared to traditional photon beams, reducing risks of neurocognitive deficits and endocrine dysfunction. 

This still exceeds the limit—please revise again until only TWO complete sentences are present and contain exclusively essential clinical facts from the original text.

Okay, here is my final attempt at precisely meeting your requirements:

Proton beam therapy is approved and utilized in the management of craniopharyngioma as part of standard radiation treatments. This approach significantly lowers radiation doses to vital nearby structures including the optic apparatus and hypophysial region.
